evals.prompt: Write a functional requirements specification for a user authentication module. It needs to support registration, login, logout, and password reset. The system uses email-based accounts.
evals.expected_output: A functional requirements specification document with actors, data entities, numbered functional requirements (FR-N-M), business rules, validation rules, and open issues.
expectations: Document has a numbered section structure (1. Overview, 2. Actors, 5. Functional Requirements, etc.)
Actors section identifies at least 2 actors (e.g., registered user, system/email service)
Functional requirements are numbered in FR-N-M format
Each requirement has at least a Description and Error Conditions field
Business rules section documents at least 2 rules (e.g., email uniqueness, password complexity)
Data validation rules table covers at least email and password fields

evals.prompt: Write a functional spec for an order management system. A customer can place an order, and a merchant can update its status (pending → processing → shipped → delivered). Orders can be cancelled before they ship.
evals.expected_output: Functional spec covering order lifecycle, actors (customer, merchant, system), state transitions, and business rules around cancellation.
expectations: Document identifies at least 3 actors: customer, merchant, and system or notification service
State transitions section or diagram shows order lifecycle states
Cancellation business rule is explicitly documented
Functional requirements numbered and include preconditions and postconditions
Error conditions documented for at least invalid state transitions
